expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: modern grid banding scenarios distinguish styling variants, interactions, and backward-compatible rendering outcomes
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly structures coverage into three sections: "Banding — feature parity & rendering outcomes" (styling variants like "Row banding — color formatting", "Column banding — color formatting", swap A/B, header-based application modes), "Banding — interactions" (scrolling, horizontal scroll, resize, sort, layout toggle), and "Banding — backward-compatible rendering outcomes" ("Legacy Report parity check" for row/column and "Parity under header-based application modes"). execution_notes.md also states: "Case focus explicitly covered: Styling variants... Interactions... Backward-compatible rendering outcomes..."

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: execution_notes.md asserts "Primary phase alignment: **phase4a**" and describes phase4a traits: "Subcategory-first structure only" and "Atomic nested steps with observable verification leaves." result.md matches this: it is a subcategory-only QA plan draft with nested steps and explicit observable outcomes (e.g., "Banding remains aligned...", "No flicker...", "Modern Grid row banding outcome matches the legacy baseline").
